hero.question: Will Sam Burns win the 2026 Masters tournament?
hero.prob_take: At 6.9%, the crowd has basically written off Sam Burns, pricing him as a fringe longshot who won't contend in Augusta in 18 months. Burns has the talent and track record to win major championships, but he's had injury issues and inconsistency that justify being a 14-to-1 underdog against a field that includes multiple players trending hot right now.

daily_take.headline: Sam Burns' Masters odds crater 6 points in one day. Here's why you should care.
daily_take.deck: Burns dropped from 13% to 6.9% overnight, a brutal washout on what looks like noise rather than fundamental reassessment. Meanwhile, Rory McIlroy rallied 18 points to 24.5%, suggesting the market is recalibrating the favorite pool ahead of 2026. Smart money is rotating out of Burns' perceived overvaluation into McIlroy's relative discount. If Burns falls below 5%, the capitulation becomes real.

sidebar.headline: Ceasefire odds jumped 13 points in 24 hours. Something happened on the ground that markets priced in fast.
sidebar.label: Israel x Hezbollah ceasefire by April 30, 2026: 41.2%

sidebar.headline: McIlroy's 18-point surge suggests Burns' collapse is a rotation play, not a fundamental shock. Check if this repricing holds or reverts.
sidebar.label: Rory McIlroy 2026 Masters: 24.5%

sidebar.headline: Warsh at 94.7% is now so certain that volume dried up to nothing. The market has spoken. No edge left here.
sidebar.label: Kevin Warsh Fed Chair confirmation: 94.7%
